'''Joshua Jones''' (born 12 May 1993) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward.

He started his professional career at [[Super League]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], with whom he won the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]]. He spent time on loan from Saints at [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[York City Knights]]. After a brief spell playing [[rugby union]] for the [[Exeter Chiefs]] in the [[English Rugby Premiership]], and time on loan at [[Taunton Rugby Football Club|Taunton]], Jones returned to rugby league with the [[Salford Red Devils]], featuring for the club in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]]. He then briefly played for [[Hull FC|Hull]] before finishing his career at [[Huddersfield Giants]].

Jones represented [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level, and was capped three times during the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour|2019 Lions tour]].

==Background==
Jones was born in [[Leyland, Lancashire|Leyland]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==Early career==
Jones signed for St Helens as a 16-year-old in 2009 from [[Blackbrook A.R.L.F.C.|Blackbrook Royals]], after previously playing for [[Chorley|Chorley Panthers]] and [[Leyland, Lancashire|Leyland Warriors]]. He has international honours from his youth rugby days; playing for [[England national rugby league team|England]] under 16s against [[France national rugby league team|France]] Schoolboys in 2009.<ref name="St Helens Profile">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/team|title=St Helens Profile|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2011-04-30|access-date=2011-04-30}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
In June 2012, Jones signed a three-year contract with St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18355439|title= Josh Jones|access-date=2012-06-09}}</ref>

St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and Jones was selected to play at centre in their 14-6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref>

===Exeter Chiefs===
On 17 June 2015 it was announced that Jones would be switching to [[rugby union]] to play for [[Aviva Premiership]] club [[Exeter Chiefs]] for the 2015-16 season.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Critchley|first1=Mike|title=Josh Jones to switch codes with Exeter Chiefs at the end of the season|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/13336445.Josh_Jones_to_switch_codes_with_Exeter_Chiefs_at_the_end_of_the_season|website=St Helens Star|access-date=17 June 2015}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
However Jones' spell in rugby union did not last long, citing personal reasons, where he switched back to [[rugby league]] for the start of the 2016 Super League season, signing for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/10130352/josh-jones-returns-to-super-league-with-salford|publisher=Sky Sports|title=Josh Jones returns to league code with Salford Red Devils|date=14 January 2016|access-date=17 January 2016}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
Jones played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 2020 season.

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 24 December 2020 it was announced that Jones would join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 Dec 2020|access-date=24 Dec 2020|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55435947|title=Huddersfield Giants: Josh Jones and Luke Yates join Super League club}}</ref>
In round 15 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he was sent off after the full-time siren for fighting in Huddersfield's 40-26 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-hull/55652/|title= Four tries from Jermaine McGillvary help Huddersfield Giants to 40-26 win over Hull FC|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, he played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809/|title=Challenge Cup player ratings as numerous stars shine in enthralling contest|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=29 May 2022|archive-date=3 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220603014903/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Jones was kept sidelined for most of the 2023 season due to concussion-related issues. In September 2023, Jones was released by Huddersfield,<ref>{{cite web |title=Josh Jones: Huddersfield Giants release Great Britain back-rower amid concussion-related issues |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66779179 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=28 December 2023 |date=11 September 2023}}</ref> and he announced his retirement shortly afterwards.<ref>{{cite web |title=Josh Jones: Former Great Britain, St Helens & Salford back-rower retires due to concussion issues |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66858576 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=28 December 2023 |date=19 September 2023}}</ref>

==International==
He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
